bullets: Lotte closed PP yarn H5300 at 1,040 CIF, clearing stock ahead of a plant merger.
Korean cargo now cheaper than Chinese (homo 1,180-1,200 CIF, little changed).
Domestic NSRP 114G low 32,500 (north to 32,000); copo trimmed to 36,500 VND/kg.

PE.sub: Festival pause before next leg down
bullets: Petrochina LLDPE deals to 1,020-1,030 CIF, 70-80 below offer.
Cynpol US offers very low (118A 960) but far-forward (~Oct), so buyers hold back.
Duanwu holiday keeps offers firm; sharper cuts expected early next week.
TPC Vina July SG660/SP660 down 3,000 to 20,000 ex-VAT (~21,600-22,032 incl VAT).
AGC Vina PM66R to 19,700-20,000 ex-VAT; large buyers bidding ~19,500.
Platts China 750 / SEA 725 CFR; oil-based domestic 22,500-23,000 VND/kg.

regional.l: Lotte PP Yarn H5300 to VN

editorial.changedNote: 10 of 50 moved down, led by PP

summary: Cynpol (US) offered far-forward cargo cheap (LLDPE 118A just 960 CIF) but ~Oct arrival keeps buyers cautious.
Petrochina LLDPE deals fell to 1,020-1,030 CIF, 70-80 below offer; Chinese LLDPE Film spreads 1,115-1,213.
Duanwu festival (today to week-end) is holding offers up; participants expect sharper cuts early next week.
paras: Today, June 19, a distributor offered LDPE Film 0423F (MI 4) from Cynpol (US) to Vietnam at 1,220 USD/ton/CIF, and offered Cynpol's HDPE injection at 1,000 USD/ton/CIF. The distributor also offered additive-free LLDPE Film (grades MI 1, MI 2) from Cynpol (US) at 1,000 USD/ton/CIF, and offered LLDPE Film 118A (MI 1, additive-free) of US origin at a very low 960 USD/ton/CIF — but these are far-forward shipments, arriving around October 2026. As a result, buyers remain cautious given the high risk.

For Chinese cargo, early this week Petrochina's LLDPE Film offer to Vietnam was revised to 1,100 USD/ton/CIF, down 20–30 USD/ton from the previous week. Today, sources said some Vietnamese buyers secured this cargo at 1,020–1,030 USD/ton/CIF, as much as 70–80 USD/ton below the offer price.

LLDPE Film offers from Chinese producers to Vietnam show a fairly wide spread: Baofeng's LLDPE Film 7042 at 1,120 USD/ton/CIF; Ningmei's LLDPE 7042 at 1,115 USD/ton/CIF; Kunlun's LLDPE 7042N at 1,157 USD/ton/CIF; and Yulong's LLDPE 7042N at 1,213 USD/ton/CIF. Distributors are holding offers high as they have entered the Duanwu (Dragon Boat) Festival holiday (running from today through the end of the week); some market participants expect offers to fall significantly at the start of next week.

Domestically, LSP has nearly run out of PP and PE inventory for local sale after a month-long production shutdown, mainly due to stepped-up exports over the recent period. This week, LSP has only a small volume of HDPE blow left, offered at a high 38,800 VND/kg incl. VAT in the south and 39,500 VND/kg incl. VAT in the north. These prices have barely transacted. Some trading sellers still hold LSP's PE cargo on the market and are closing deals at much lower prices. In addition, LSP's distribution office is also offering HDPE of Chinese origin.

On the market, offer-price gaps between distributors are also fairly wide. Mid-week, one distributor offered SK Chemical (Korea) HDPE yarn 3301 at 36,500 VND/kg incl. VAT, and Lotte Hyundai (Korea) HDPE yarn MF5000 at 37,500 VND/kg incl. VAT — down 500 VND/kg from late last week.

Middle East-origin HDPE Film is commonly offered in the 37,000–38,000 VND/kg incl. VAT range. Notably, one large customer shared that it secured STL (China) HDPE FS7000 at just 34,800 VND/kg incl. VAT, though most smaller customers are still being offered this cargo at around 36,000 VND/kg incl. VAT.

For LDPE, a top-tier distributor is offering Lotte Titan (Malaysia) LDPE Film 260GG at 45,000 VND/kg incl. VAT, below most offers from other trading companies. Petrochina (China) LDPE 2426H is commonly offered at around 44,000–44,500 VND/kg incl. VAT, though in practice some sellers have been “dumping” at just 40,000–40,500 VND/kg incl. VAT.

For LLDPE, even premium Middle East-origin grades such as Sabic's 218WJ and Qamar's FD21HS were recorded transacting at 35,000 VND/kg incl. VAT today; Chinese-origin LLDPE Film was offered by one seller at 34,500 VND/kg incl. VAT, but in practice has been transacting at around 33,000 VND/kg incl. VAT or lower since last week.

Across the wider region, per Platts assessments in Northeast Asia, HDPE Film/yarn/injection/blow prices all fell 20 USD/ton week-on-week this week, LLDPE fell 30 USD/ton, and LDPE fell 40 USD/ton. In Southeast Asia, all HDPE grades fell 60 USD/ton week-on-week to 1,230–1,240 USD/ton/CFR; LLDPE fell 60 USD/ton to 1,190 USD/ton/CFR; LLDPE metallocene C6 fell 60 USD/ton to 1,340 USD/ton/CFR; and LDPE also fell 60 USD/ton to 1,440 USD/ton/CFR.

Asia's PE market fell fairly sharply this week on the steep drop in crude oil prices and limited demand. Particularly in Southeast Asia, price competition remains high amid more abundant supply, with many offers recorded from Korea and Southeast Asia in addition to Chinese cargo. Notably, Prefchem (Malaysia)'s PE complex is expected to restart operations late this June, which could add further supply pressure.

summary: Lotte Hyundai closed PP yarn H5300 at just 1,040 CIF — below Chinese cargo — clearing stock ahead of a plant merger.
Chinese homo (Jinneng/Petrochina/Sinopec) 1,180-1,200 CIF, little changed; Korean cargo now the most competitive.
Domestic: NSRP 114G low 32,500 (north deals to 32,000); BSR T3034 33,800-34,000; copo cut to 36,500 VND/kg.
paras: Early this week, Lotte Hyundai (Korea)'s settled PP yarn sale price to Vietnamese buyers was widely reported at 1,170 USD/ton/CIF.

However today, June 19, several market sources said Lotte closed a 500-ton order of PP yarn grade H5300 at just 1,040 USD/ton/CIF, and buyers taking larger volumes could even negotiate 10–20 USD/ton lower. According to sources, Lotte Hyundai has recently cut prices on some PP and PE grades to very low levels — below even Chinese cargo — to clear inventory ahead of a plant merger.

At present, Korean-origin PP offered into Vietnam is extremely price-competitive, cheaper than Chinese-origin cargo. Aggregating sources, PP homo offers from Chinese producers such as Jinneng, Petrochina and Sinopec to Vietnam are ranging at 1,180–1,200 USD/ton/CIF, little changed from the previous week.

Domestically, Hyosung Vina today still held its PP yarn/injection offer at a high 35,200 VND/kg incl. VAT in the south. This is a hard-to-transact level, as many other PP homo grades — including NSRP and BSR domestic cargo — are up to 1,500–2,500 VND/kg cheaper. The main reason is that Hyosung Vina produces PP homo in low volumes and prioritizes sales to its traditional customers. In practice in the south, BSR's PP T3034 has transacted at 33,800–34,000 VND/kg incl. VAT.

In the north, some sources said the market has recorded PP homo deals at a low of 32,000 VND/kg incl. VAT. However, a quick survey found NSRP's PP 114G at a lowest transacted price of 32,500 VND/kg incl. VAT, 300 VND/kg below the lowest settled price early in the week. Korean-origin off-grade PP homo (OG) is offered in the 30,000–31,000 VND/kg incl. VAT range depending on grade, with buyers only bidding at 29,000 VND/kg incl. VAT or below.

While PP homo has yet to fall much further, PP copo has seen some sellers cut prices more. For example, mid-week some sellers offered Hanwha (Korea)-origin PP copo BJ350 and Exxon Mobil-origin PP copo 7032E3 at 37,000 VND/kg incl. VAT, but today a distributor was recorded offering this cargo at 36,500 VND/kg incl. VAT — a further 500 VND/kg cut.

Domestic PP trading remains fairly cautious as many buyers wait for further price drops. Supply is fairly abundant. Domestically, apart from LSP, which has shut down and temporarily halted production, the remaining PP plants are all running at high rates — reportedly the three plants NSRP, BSR and Hyosung Vina are all operating at 100% capacity — leading to oversupply. This has left buyers indifferent to imports, especially PP homo grades.

Across the wider region, per Platts assessments in Northeast Asia, PP yarn/injection/IPP prices fell by up to 70 USD/ton week-on-week this week, BOPP fell 50 USD/ton, and PP block copo fell sharply by 130 USD/ton. In Southeast Asia, the declines were smaller (as the prior two weeks had already fallen sharply), with PP yarn/injection down 20 USD/ton week-on-week to 1,180 USD/ton/CFR; BOPP down 20 USD/ton to 1,200 USD/ton/CFR; IPP Film down 40 USD/ton to 1,230 USD/ton/CFR; while PP block copo rose 20 USD/ton to 1,230 USD/ton/CFR (after a very steep assessment drop the prior week).

Per another international organization's assessments, all PP grades in Northeast Asia fell 20 USD/ton week-on-week this week; prices in Southeast Asia fell sharply by 40–50 USD/ton; in India PP prices held flat over the week, while other South Asian countries all recorded declines of 20–30 USD/ton.

PP prices in Asia weakened this week on the impact of more favorable developments in the Middle East as the US and Iran reached a peace agreement, which dragged crude oil prices sharply lower. PP supply is also improving as some Asian plants restart earlier than expected following maintenance.

summary: Platts: China PVC -10 to 750, SEA -35 to 725 CFR; another assessment puts SEA at 770-790 amid -20 cuts.
TPC Vina's July offer cut SG660/SP660 3,000 to 20,000 ex-VAT (21,600-22,032 incl VAT) for large buyers.
AGC Vina PM66R to 19,700-20,000 ex-VAT; Yihua SG-5 21,500; oil-based commonly 22,500-23,000 VND/kg.
paras: Per Platts assessments, PVC prices in Asia continued to fall this week on weak demand and high inventory. Specifically, China's PVC price fell 10 USD/ton week-on-week to 750 USD/ton/CFR; the PVC price in Southeast Asia fell 35 USD/ton to 725 USD/ton/CFR.

Per another international organization's assessments, PVC prices across Asian regions all fell 20 USD/ton week-on-week this week (Southeast Asia PVC at 770–790 USD/ton/CFR). With ample inventory, buyers are in no hurry to purchase. Conversely, suppliers are facing significant selling pressure.

This week, China's overall PVC operating rate was recorded at about 69.47%, up 1.68 percentage points from the previous week. Of this, the carbide-based PVC operating rate rose 0.95 percentage points week-on-week to 77.62%, while the ethylene-based PVC operating rate rose 3.37 percentage points to 50.27%. In Korea, Hanwha Solutions cut its PVC operating rate — covering both suspension and paste resin — from about 80% to 60%–70%, starting late last week.

In Vietnam, producer TPC Vina offered PVC for July 2026 delivery to the domestic market, with its PVC SG660/SP660 offer to large customers down sharply by 3,000 VND/kg from the prior month to 20,000 VND/kg excl. VAT; offers to medium and small customers at 20,200–20,400 VND/kg excl. VAT. Including the full 8% VAT, offers range from 21,600–22,032 VND/kg incl. VAT.

Early this week, producer AGC Vina (ACVN) offered PVC for July 2026 delivery to the domestic market. Its PVC PM66R offer was 20,400–20,600 VND/kg excl. VAT for medium-to-small customers (depending on volume), and 20,200 VND/kg excl. VAT for the largest customers, down sharply by 2,500–2,800 VND/kg. Mid-week, distributors cut offers to 19,700–20,000 VND/kg excl. VAT, down 600 VND/kg from early in the week. Large customers could even bid at 19,500 VND/kg incl. VAT.

On the trading market, a leading distributor is still offering Yihua's PVC SG-5 at 21,500 VND/kg incl. VAT in Yen Vien commune (Hanoi). Meanwhile, oil-based PVC continues to see many sellers cut prices sharply, bringing prices down commonly to 22,500–23,000 VND/kg incl. VAT — only about 1,000 VND/kg above coal-based PVC. One large distributor even offered AGC Vinythai (Thailand)-origin PVC 266RC down to 22,200 VND/kg incl. VAT, competing with coal-based PVC of other origins.
